core_mechanism: characterizing the long-run statistics of a chaotic dissipative dynamical system by its Sinai-Ruelle-Bowen (SRB) invariant measure — the physically-selected stationary distribution on a strange attractor — and deriving falsifiable predictions from that measure without constructing it explicitly
brief_reason: The abstract applies Ruelle's principle / SRB distribution (the natural invariant measure of a chaotic attractor) to a many-particle nonequilibrium stationary state to extract a specific prediction without building the SRB itself, then tests it against a computer experiment.
